description_long: This is a brightly colored biplane with two blue straight wings stacked vertically, each adorned with roundels resembling the Italian Air Force insignia. The fuselage is painted blue with a wooden brown nose and a green, white, and red striped tail fin, evoking the Italian flag. The front features a large, exposed radial piston engine with a wooden propeller and two smaller propellers mounted on the lower wings. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a single seat and visible control stick. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail wheel, all fixed and non-retractable. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century military biplanes, with a playful, stylized aesthetic.
description: A colorful, stylized biplane with blue wings, Italian roundels, a wooden propeller, and an open cockpit. The tail is striped green, white, and red, and the landing gear is fixed.
description_view_0: Side view showing the stacked blue wings, open cockpit, and the Italian tricolor tail fin.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the blue wings with roundels, the open cockpit, and the symmetrical wing structure.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same wing and tail features, with the propeller and engine visible at the front.
description_view_3: Front view showing the large wooden propeller, exposed radial engine, and the two smaller propellers on the lower wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted radial piston engine with a wooden propeller; two small auxiliary propellers on lower wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; tail fin painted in tricolor.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a small tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
